$schema-note: Per-repo config for the pi harness engine. The Verifier (run_check) and the inline /checks command may run ONLY the named checks defined here plus the always-available git checks (git-diff, git-diff-stat, git-status, git-log). Each check maps to a FIXED argv vector run with shell:false (no shell, no chaining). 'boundaries' is the machine form of AGENTS.md's 'Boundaries (do not touch without asking)' — JS regex strings matched REPO-ROOT-relative by the command-guard extension to block writes/edits into those paths (keep it in sync with the AGENTS.md prose). Secret redaction patterns live separately in harness/redaction.json (optional). Replace the example checks below with your project's real commands. See harness/examples/checks.python-lmcache.json for a complete Python example.

args: replace with your test command, e.g. pytest -q
replace with your lint command, e.g. ruff check .

$experiments-note: Closed allowlist of long-lived experiments the /monitor sub-agent may launch via run_experiment. Each maps to a FIXED argv (cmd/args/timeoutMs) run with shell:false; the model picks a NAME, never a command. NO logFile field — the log path is per-RUN (memory/runs/<runId>.log). Replace the example below with your project's real benchmarks/long runs.

args: replace with your experiment command, e.g. python bench/run.py --config configs/smoke.yaml

$autoJudge-note: Optional config for the auto-judge extension (harness/pi/auto-judge): an LLM-as-judge gate that, when ARMED in a session (/autojudge on — OFF by default, and dormant unless this block exists), asks a judge model to ALLOW or DENY each guarded tool call before it runs, fail-closed. Fields: judgeModel ("" resolves to the review/adversarial-judge class GPT-5.5 per the model policy — set a pi model id to override); guardedTools (tool names to gate; default ["bash","write","edit"]); failClosed (true = block when the judge errors or times out; false is debug-only and discouraged); timeoutMs (judge time budget in ms; must be >0 and ≤120000, else default 20000); contextDiff (when true, include the working-tree git diff as extra context); policy (the natural-language rules the judge enforces). Each guarded call spawns a judge subprocess and BLOCKS the session until it replies, so keep the policy tight and arm it only when you want that cost. Main-session only — sub-agents run --no-extensions and are never gated. The model-driven spawn tools — delegate, workflow, and the six dual-mode role tools subagent_plan/subagent_verify/subagent_triage/subagent_monitor/subagent_report/subagent_research — are listed here so that, WHEN ARMED, each model-initiated spawn is judged. The six role tools have no hard count cap, so /autojudge on is their only gate; delegate and workflow ALSO carry their own per-request caps (maxCallsPerRequest / maxWorkflowsPerRequest), but guarding them here adds the content gate the caps don't (gating stays auto-judge-only by owner decision — no per-call/cost cap beyond those, no dark-by-default toggle). The match is EXACT (Array.includes), so these strings must equal the registered tool names verbatim — a typo silently un-gates. contextDiff stays false for them: arming already ≈ doubles spawns, and a per-spawn working-tree diff would compound cost for no safety gain on these isolated, edit-free children.

autoJudge.policy: Allow routine, in-scope, reversible actions. DENY: destructive or irreversible commands (e.g. rm -rf, git push --force, git reset --hard, history rewrites, dropping or truncating data); anything that exfiltrates secrets or sends repo contents to an external service; and edits to files outside the current task's stated scope. When in doubt, deny.

$delegate-note: Optional config for the delegate extension (harness/pi/delegate): a model-callable, isolated, READ-ONLY subagent (tools read,grep,find,ls) that the MAIN-session model can spawn mid-turn via delegate({prompt}) — the pi analog of Claude Code's Task/Agent tool. The PRESENCE of this block (even {}) opts the tool in; with no block it is inert (refuses). Fields: maxCallsPerRequest (spawns allowed per main-agent request, reset each request; default 3, clamped [1,20]); confirmOnSpawn (when an interactive UI is present, ask before spawning so a human can catch an injected prompt; default true — headless has NO such gate, so add "delegate" to autoJudge.guardedTools above + arm /autojudge for the headless gate, as done here); model/effort ("" = the worker default Opus 4.8 / xhigh per the model policy — set a pi model id / thinking level to override); capBytes (byte cap on the returned text; default 16384, clamped [1,262144]). Read-only ⇒ no mutation, but built-in read takes ABSOLUTE paths and cannot be path-jailed (out-of-repo reads like ~/.ssh are a documented residual), and the returned text is untrusted DATA — never instructions. Main-session only: workers run --no-extensions so they can't re-enter delegate (recursion bounded at depth 1).
$workflow-note: Optional config for the workflow extension (harness/pi/workflow): a model-callable GOVERNED parallel fan-out. The MAIN-session model passes workflow({objective, tasks[]}); the tool right-sizes the fan-out (a hard cap PLUS an optional LLM right-sizer that prunes/merges overlapping tasks) and runs the kept tasks as N isolated READ-ONLY subagents (read,grep,find,ls), writing each result — REDACTED AT THE SOURCE — to memory/workflow/<runId>/<i>-<slug>.md and returning a compact index. PRESENCE of this block (even {}) opts it in; absent = inert. Fields: maxParallel (hard cap on kept tasks / total spawns — the cost-governor KILL-SWITCH; default 5, clamped [1,8]); concurrency (workers running at once; default 5, clamped [1,maxParallel]); maxWorkflowsPerRequest (fan-outs per main-agent request, reset each request; default 2, clamped [1,10]); useJudge (run the LLM right-sizer; default true, but it only fires at tasks.length >= judgeThreshold); judgeThreshold (default 2*maxParallel); judgeModel ("" = the review/adversarial-judge class GPT-5.5 per the model policy — set a pi model id to override); synthesize (run one extra subagent to consolidate the already-redacted results; default false); maxInputTasks (cap incoming tasks[] BEFORE the right-sizer so a huge array can't bloat its prompt; default 30, clamped [1,200]); maxResultBytes (per-worker result-file byte cap; default 32768, clamped [1,262144]); timeoutMs (per-worker wall-clock; default 600000, clamped [1,1800000]). The right-sizer is a COST gate, NOT a safety gate — it prunes overlap and fails OPEN to the clamp; task-content safety rests on "workflow" in autoJudge.guardedTools above (which gates the fan-out spawn) + the per-worker read residual. Read-only ⇒ no mutation, but built-in read can't be path-jailed (out-of-repo reads are a documented residual), and worker text is untrusted DATA. Workers run --no-extensions ⇒ no recursive fan-out.
